channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Estados rejeitam pedido de Lula para reduzir ICMS sobre o diesel
item.description: State governments rejected President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's request to reduce the ICMS tax on diesel, in response to rising international oil prices due to the war in Iran. The decision reflects states' reluctance to forego tax revenue, despite federal pressure to mitigate the inflationary impact of fuel costs.

item.title: Candidatos às prefeituras francesas anunciam alianças e redesenham cenário político do país
item.description: Candidates in French municipal elections are announcing alliances for the runoff, reshaping the country's political landscape. Following the conclusion of the first-round vote count, parties have until Tuesday night (17th) to finalize their tickets, aiming to consolidate support and prevent fragmentation. This reflects a standard strategy in two-round electoral systems, where post-first-round agreements are often decisive for victory.

item.title: Quaest: 56% dizem já ter definido voto para presidente; 43% afirmam que ainda podem mudar
item.description: The Genial/Quaest poll reveals that 56% of Brazilians have already decided their presidential vote for 2026, while 43% say they could still change. Among Lula (PT) supporters, 67% are firm in their choice, and among Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) supporters, 63% are decided. Women are evenly split (49% decided, 49% undecided), whereas men show stronger conviction (62% decided, 37% undecided).

item.title: Homens e mulheres devem lutar pela superação de uma sociedade perversa, defende ministra Cármen Lúcia
item.description: TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia urged men and women to jointly fight against a perverse and unconstitutional society regarding women, citing a 13% increase in femicides in 2025. She stated that violence against women denies democracy and advocated using the Law to ensure justice and peace, referencing the Maria da Penha Law as an essential model.

item.title: Lulinha soa sem pé nem cabeça
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís da Silva, known as Lulinha, was criticized by Josias de Souza as weak and incoherent, based on claims that he has no ties to businessman Careca do INSS, that the introduction was made by a friend without any agreements, and that the trip to Portugal to visit a medicinal cannabis farm was motivated by family concerns over his niece’s epilepsy. The author argues that, although the defense attempts to sow doubt in the investigation, it lacks credibility with voters and resembles an implausible fable. Lula, by refusing to defend his son, appears to have acted knowingly.

item.title: A disputa entre Furlan e Clécio pelo governo do Amapá, segundo o Paraná Pesquisas
item.description: Paraná Pesquisas released a survey showing Dr. Furlan (PSD), former mayor of Macapá, leading the race for governor of Amapá with 65.1%, while incumbent governor Clécio Luís (União Brasil) trails at 25.3%. In the Senate race, Rayssa Furlan (Podemos), Furlan’s wife, leads both scenarios tested, reaching up to 66.1% of mentions, indicating strong influence of the Furlan family in Amapá politics.

item.title: A colher de chá de Lula a Motta para evitar problemas a projeto prioritário
item.description: President Lula decided not to send a constitutional urgency bill to end the 6x1 work scale, aiming to avoid conflict with Chamber President Hugo Motta and preserve their positive relationship. Instead, he backed the ongoing PEC in the Chamber’s CCJ, allowing Motta to lead the process and aim for approval before the parliamentary recess. This move reflects a political strategy to advance a key government priority without triggering institutional tensions.

item.title: CEO do Itaú é favorito para presidir conselho diretor da Febraban
item.description: Milton Maluhy Filho, CEO of Itaú, is the sole candidate for president of the Febraban board of directors for the 2026–2029 term, with election set for April 9. Current president Luiz Carlos Trabuco will step down after the vote, per entity protocol. Febraban also announced Nubank as a new member following Maluhy Filho’s endorsement, as the fintech seeks a banking license after becoming Brazil’s largest private financial institution by customer count.

item.title: Eleições 2026: A difícil situação do aliado de Alcolumbre na disputa pela reeleição no Amapá
item.description: Amapá Governor Clécio Luis (União Brasil) is trailing in the re-election race with 25.3% voter intention against Dr. Furlan (PSD) at 65.1%, according to Paraná Pesquisas. His key ally, Senate President Davi Alcolumbre (União Brasil), is under investigation by the Electoral Public Ministry for alleged premature campaigning during the inauguration of the Amapá Radiotherapy Center, though a request to remove related digital content was denied by the TRE-AP president.

item.title: AP: Rayssa e Barreto lideram corrida pelo senado, enquanto Randolfe fica em terceiro
item.description: Rayssa Furlan (Podemos) leads the Senate race in Amapá with 63.8% voting intention, followed by Lucas Barreto (PSD) at 46.2% and Randolfe Rodrigues (PT) at 39%. Even without MDB's Acácio Favacho, Rayssa and Barreto remain ahead, while PDT's Waldez Góes has the highest rejection rate at 46.1%.

item.title: Bolsonaro quer Zema como vice de Flávio
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro, before being hospitalized for bilateral bacterial pneumonia, asked congressional allies to convince Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) to run as vice president on Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL) 2026 presidential ticket. The request was made during visits Bolsonaro received while hospitalized.

item.title: José Dirceu deve reunir políticos em festa de 80 anos em Brasília
item.description: José Dirceu, former Chief of Staff and PT founder, celebrated his 80th birthday with events in Brasília and São Paulo, gathering politicians across the spectrum and reinforcing his pre-candidacy for federal deputy from São Paulo in 2026, at Lula’s invitation. He criticized Tarcísio de Freitas and Flávio Bolsonaro, warned of threats to national sovereignty, and noted that the annulment of his Lava Jato convictions restored his political rights. The PT aims to leverage his candidacy to strengthen its congressional bench in 2026.

item.title: Retorno da Petrobras à distribuição é discurso eleitoreiro, diz setor
item.description: The fuel distribution sector claims that government statements about Petrobras returning to fuel retail are electoral rhetoric for 2026, as the privatization of BR Distribuidora includes a non-compete clause valid until 2029, preventing the state company from operating in the segment before then without renegotiation or additional costs.

item.title: Governo federal vê trechos inconstitucionais no PL Antifacação, mas veto causaria desgaste a Lula
item.description: Federal government technicians identify potentially unconstitutional provisions in the Antifacção bill, particularly the ban on voting for pretrial detainees and cuts to prison allowance, but assess that a presidential veto would cause political damage to Lula due to public sensitivity on security and the risk of legislative backlash, following last-minute negotiations by the Justice Ministry.

item.title: PF apontou que mudança de Lulinha ao exterior poderia ser ‘evasão do País’ por investigação do INSS
item.description: A Federal Police report suggests that Fábio Luís Lula da Silva's move abroad may have been an attempt to evade investigations into alleged fraud at the INSS, linked to businessman Antônio Camilo Antunes ('Careca do INSS'). The PF alleges that a medicinal cannabis project with Antunes was intended to launder funds from the pension scheme fraud, though Lulinha's defense claims he moved before the operation and received no funds. While Lulinha admitted a sporadic social relationship with Antunes, a witness claimed monthly payments of R$300k — not found in bank records — which the defense denies.

item.title: Haddad deixa o Ministério da Fazenda nesta semana; veja os principais marcos da gestão
item.description: Fernando Haddad resigns as Minister of Finance after three years and two months to run for governor of São Paulo for the PT in 2026. His tenure was marked by the approval of the consumption tax reform and the creation of the fiscal framework, though his agenda was constrained by political limitations within the government. While economic indicators such as GDP, unemployment, and income improved, Haddad failed to eliminate the public deficit or project a sustainable decline in public debt, leaving a legacy of technical skill but limited impact due to internal government constraints.

item.title: Interferência estrangeira nas eleições do Brasil é preocupante, alerta entidade intergovernamental
item.description: Marcela Rios Tobar, director for Latin America at International Idea, warns of concerns over possible foreign interference in Brazil’s 2026 elections, particularly from the United States, citing active influence attempts in the region. She acknowledges Brazil is better prepared in 2026 than in 2022 due to proactive actions by the TSE, but stresses that rising disinformation—driven by AI and weakened social media moderation—heightens voter vulnerability amid widespread public distrust in institutions.

item.title: Michelle exalta ator que venceu Wagner Moura no Oscar
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro praised actor Michael B. Jordan for winning the 2026 Oscar for Best Actor for his role in 'Pecadores', highlighting his speech thanking God and his family. Jordan competed against Wagner Moura, who was nominated for 'The Secret Agent', along with Timothée Chalamet and Leonardo DiCaprio. The post drew criticism from journalists, who filed a police complaint over its perceived inappropriateness.

item.title: Lula deve assinar decreto que regulamenta ECA Digital nesta terça; veja principais pontos
item.description: President Lula is set to sign decrees regulating the Digital ECA, banning manipulative practices such as infinite scroll, autoplay videos, and reward systems on digital platforms to protect children and adolescents. The decrees also establish a Federal Police complaint center and restructure the ANPD to enforce the law, which takes effect this Tuesday.

item.title: Para paulistas, governo de SP e concessionárias são principais responsáveis por quantidade de pedágios, diz Datafolha
item.description: According to a Datafolha poll, São Paulo residents primarily blame the state government of Tarcísio de Freitas and highway concessionaires for the high number of tolls in the state, with 33% and 30% of responses, respectively. The federal government was cited by 26%, technically tied with concessionaires within the survey’s two-point margin of error. Responsibility attributions vary by income, education, gender, and political leaning, with higher blame on the state government among wealthier respondents, those with higher education, and those who evaluate the governor’s performance negatively.

item.title: CPMI do INSS não conseguiu analisar os dados de Lulinha enquanto teve acesso
item.description: The INSS CPMI was unable to analyze data on Fábio Luís da Silva (Lulinha) due to the suspension of bank and fiscal secrecy by Supreme Court Minister Flávio Dino, according to committee president Senator Carlos Viana. He stated the decision blocked timely analysis and called it a disrespect to parliamentary work, while affirming compliance with the Court. Viana confirmed Lulinha received R$300,000 monthly from the INSS figure known as 'Careca,' but could not determine a link to pension fraud.

item.title: O Caso Master e a sombra da delação de Daniel Vorcaro - O Assunto #1681
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, banker of Banco Master, changed lawyers after the STF formed a majority for his imprisonment, hiring José Luís Oliveira Lima, an expert in plea bargains, opening the door to a potential collaboration that could implicate high-ranking figures in Brazilian politics. A Quaest poll shows 38% of voters reject voting for candidates involved in the Master Case, raising early concerns ahead of the 2026 elections, especially due to risks of STF ministers and right-wing politicians being implicated. Claudio Couto, political scientist at FGV-SP, warns that any plea deal must follow strict judicial procedures and could significantly impact the electoral landscape.

item.title: Valdemar Costa Neto diz que toda família Bolsonaro tomou vacina contra a covid
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL party, stated that former President Jair Bolsonaro and his entire family received the COVID-19 vaccine, despite Bolsonaro's public opposition to vaccination during the pandemic. He said he had to convince Bolsonaro's eldest son, Flávio, to get vaccinated after a 'fight' and used this as leverage, telling Bolsonaro that his refusal was harming his standing with women voters. Bolsonaro reportedly replied that he did not take the vaccine, while Costa Neto suggested this confusion about the pandemic hurt Bolsonaro's electoral performance among female voters.

item.title: ECA Digital começa a valer e impõe novas regras para crianças e jovens em redes sociais, jogos e sites
item.description: The Digital ECA, signed by President Lula in September 2024, takes effect today, imposing new rules to protect children and adolescents online. The law bans self-declared age, requires linking accounts of minors under 16 to guardians, mandates age verification in marketplaces and betting platforms, blocks prohibited content, and demands transparency in content moderation from platforms with over one million minor users. Implementation details will be clarified by a decree expected to be published today.

item.title: Defesa confirma que Careca do INSS financiou viagem de Lulinha
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha) confirmed that lobbyist Antonio Carlos Camilo Antunes, known as 'Careca do INSS', financed a trip to Portugal in November 2024 for Lulinha to learn about cannabidiol-based medicine production. Antunes, imprisoned since September 2025 over INSS fraud investigated in Operation Sem Desconto, presented the invitation as a legitimate pharmaceutical business initiative, with no allegations against him at the time. The information was submitted to Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça, and the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Commission authorized financial data access to investigate the matter.

item.title: "Em um país sério, Moraes estaria afastado do cargo", diz Carlos Viana sobre o caso do Banco Master
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), criticized the STF and Minister Alexandre de Moraes, stating that in a serious country, Moraes would be removed from office due to alleged bias in the Banco Master case. He advocated for a CPMI to investigate the bank’s ties to the financial system and accused the Senate of inaction. The remarks were made during the Roda Viva program on TV Cultura on March 16, 2026.

item.title: Moraes deve ser afastado durante investigações sobre Master, diz Viana
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS CPMI, stated that Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes should be removed from office during investigations into alleged messages exchanged with Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Master, on the day of Vorcaro’s first arrest by the PF in November 2025. Viana said the committee accessed Vorcaro’s phone data and confirmed the recipient number belonged to a functioning STF device, though he initially attributed it to Moraes before correcting it to the Supreme Court. He said he personally verified the information and will send an official letter to the Court to clarify who was using the device on the day of the alleged communication.

item.title: Caminho para combater o crime é cooperação e não chamar de terrorismo, diz secretário nacional de Segurança
item.description: President Lula will sign the anti-factions bill and decrees to combat organized crime, focusing on financial strangulation of criminal organizations and international cooperation, including with the U.S. National Public Security Secretary Francisco Lucas advocates cooperation as the correct path, contrasting with the Trump administration's threat to label factions like PCC and CV as terrorists. The measure is also supported by right-wing politicians and is expected to be part of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's platform.

item.title: Decisão de Dino sobre punição a juízes provoca dúvidas sobre alcance e pode concentrar poder no STF
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Flávio Dino blocked the compulsory retirement of judges, but his decision applies only to the specific case in question, raising doubts about its legal scope and potential to concentrate power in the STF. Experts consulted by Folha warn the measure could set a dangerous precedent if interpreted broadly.

item.title: Veja principais pontos do ECA Digital, que entra em vigor nesta terça
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will sign on Tuesday (March 17) the decrees regulating the Digital Statute for Children and Adolescents (ECA Digital), Law No. 15.211/2025, which comes into force to protect minors from violence, abuse, and improper exposure online. The legislation extends the 1990 ECA guarantees to the virtual environment, covering social media, apps, video games, and other digital services, regardless of company location. Three decrees drafted by the Ministry of Justice and the Presidency’s Secretariat of Social Communication will make the statute operational, emphasizing prevention and effectiveness.

item.title: Flavio Bolsonaro faz dancinha enquanto o pai está internado; Assista
item.description: Flavio Bolsonaro, a presidential pre-candidate, participated in a PL event in Rondônia, where he jumped on stage and danced for his supporters, showing enthusiasm for the pre-campaign. The moment took place while his father, Jair Bolsonaro, was hospitalized.

item.title: Presidente do PT diz que partido ‘tem que ser’ antissistema e pede mobilização para 2026
item.description: PT President Edinho Silva stated that the party 'must be' anti-system and urged grassroots mobilization in the streets and on social media for the 2026 elections. He emphasized that the PT represents change and cannot retreat, reiterating a stance he previously expressed during the party's 46th anniversary in February. The remarks align with the start of the party's electoral mobilization, as Haddad is expected to announce his candidacy for governor of São Paulo during the 17th Federative Caravan, with Lula's confirmed participation.

item.title: “Podemos matar a eleição no primeiro turno”, diz Valdemar
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL party, stated that his preference for running mate with Flávio Bolsonaro is Tereza Cristina, dismissing other speculations. He also claimed it is possible to 'kill the election in the first round,' expressing confidence in an immediate victory. The remarks were made during the Frente a Frente program by Folha and TV Cultura.

item.title: Governo Lula lança plano para enfrentar crise climática e prevê orçamento de quase R$ 180 bilhões
item.description: President Lula's government launched the National Plan on Climate Change (Plano Clima) with a projected budget of nearly R$ 180 billion by 2025, including R$ 27.5 billion in repayable funds for 2026. Organized around three pillars—mitigation, adaptation, and cross-cutting strategies—the plan aims to eradicate severe food insecurity, protect 4 million people in geohydrological risk zones, make 72.68 million hectares of livestock production sustainable, and increase marine protected areas to 30% by 2035.

item.title: Governo fecha plano de R$ 27,5 bi para redução de gases poluentes
item.description: President Lula’s administration launched the Climate Plan 2024-2035, allocating R$ 27.5 billion for 2026 to cut greenhouse gas emissions and achieve net-zero by 2050. The Climate Fund, managed by BNDES and MMA, relies on repayable loans and includes five cross-cutting strategies such as just transition and transparency. Environmentalists, however, criticized the plan for allowing a 1% increase in emissions from agribusiness.

item.title: PT anuncia lançamento da pré-candidatura de Haddad ao governo de SP na sexta
item.description: The PT is set to announce Fernando Haddad as its pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo on Friday (20), in an event featuring President Lula and ministers at the São Paulo Chemical Workers’ Union. Prior to the announcement, Haddad will attend the 17th Federative Caravan (March 19-20) and a tribute to former Uruguayan president Pepe Mujica at UFABC. A Datafolha poll shows Tarcísio de Freitas leading with 44% voting intention compared to Haddad’s 31% in a first-round scenario.

item.title: Deputado do Podemos entra na disputa por vaga no TCU, que já tem cinco candidatos
item.description: Federal Deputy Gilson Daniel (Podemos-ES) officially announced his candidacy for the vacant position at the TCU following the compulsory retirement of Minister Aroldo Cedraz in February 2026. The race already includes five other formally registered candidates. Appointment to the TCU is made by the National Congress upon presidential nomination, subject to Senate approval.

item.title: Valdemar fala sobre vice de Flávio, prisão de Bolsonaro e fim da 6x1
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL party, announced that Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) will be the vice-presidential candidate on Flávio Bolsonaro’s (PL-RJ) ticket for the 2026 elections. He also commented on Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation and the end of the 6x1 work schedule, emphasizing the need for labor reforms.

item.title: Haja, coração: em campo, na Copa de 2026, a pior seleção brasileira da história
item.description: The Brazilian national team is experiencing its worst historical moment ahead of the 2026 World Cup, with a squad deemed weak across all positions — especially midfield (Casemiro, Andrey, Fabinho) and attack (Mateus Cunha, Igor Thiago, João Pedro) — despite widespread support for Italian coach Carlo Ancelotti. The article contrasts this lineup with past generations, emphasizing the lack of true stars and the reliance on a miracle, such as Neymar’s potential return, to avoid failure. Remaining hope rests on the 'supernatural of Almeida,' a concept from Nelson Rodrigues, implying only footballing fate can save the team.

item.title: Bolívia quer usar fronteira com 5 países para distribuir energia
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz announced that Bolivia aims to become an energy distribution hub, leveraging its borders with Brazil, Chile, Peru, Paraguay, and Argentina through a new hydrocarbons law and energy exchange agreements with Brazil. The initiative seeks to boost Bolivia’s economic growth and meet regional energy demand, emphasizing cross-border cooperation and local development. In a meeting with Lula, both leaders signed pacts on security, including combating human trafficking, drug trafficking, money laundering, and environmental crimes.

item.title: Neymar tem até 16 jogos para convencer Ancelotti a levá-lo à Copa do Mundo de 2026
item.description: Neymar has 16 matches with Santos until May 18 to prove he is physically fit for the 2026 World Cup, after being omitted from friendlies against France and Croatia due to not being at 100%, according to Ancelotti. The CBF will submit a preliminary list of 55 names to FIFA on May 11, with the final list released on May 18. Despite the tight schedule, it is unlikely Neymar will play all matches due to injury prevention efforts, having already been rested in recent games.

item.title: Careca do INSS pagou viagem de Lulinha a Portugal, admite defesa ao STF
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, admitted that lobbyist Antonio Carlos Camilo Antunes, nicknamed 'Careca do INSS', paid for a trip he took to Portugal. The admission was made in the context of a case before the Supreme Federal Court (STF).

item.title: Edinho Silva diz que PT é antissistema e pede mobilização eleitoral
item.description: Edinho Silva, president of the Workers' Party (PT), stated that the party is 'anti-system' and the party of change, calling for mobilization in the streets and on social media to show strength. His remarks, made in an Instagram video, reinforce the party's discourse ahead of the 2026 elections, as preparations advance for Fernando Haddad's candidacy for governor of São Paulo.

item.title: Bolívia classifica crime organizado como terrorismo e firma pacto com Brasil
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz classified criminal organizations such as the PCC and CV as terrorism, aligning with the U.S. proposal, during a meeting with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in Brasília. He also announced a cooperation pact between Bolivia and Brazil to combat transnational organized crime.

item.title: Lulinha admite ao STF que teve viagem a Portugal bancada pelo Careca do INSS: ‘Relação esporádica’
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, the eldest son of President Lula, admitted to the STF that he took a trip to Portugal in November 2024 funded by Antônio Camilo Antunes, the 'Careca do INSS', who is under suspicion of embezzling pension funds. Lulinha claims the relationship was purely social and occasional, with no commercial ties or knowledge of wrongdoing, and that he accepted the invitation to learn about a medicinal cannabidiol project due to his niece's medical needs.

item.title: Investigaçőes contradizem explicaçőes de Javier Milei sobre escândalo com criptomoeda
item.description: An investigation by Argentina’s Public Ministry uncovered documents on the phone of businessman Mauricio Novelli suggesting a $5 million deal between Javier Milei and Hayden Davis, CEO of Kelsier Ventures, to promote the cryptocurrency $Libra. The findings contradict Milei’s claims that he had no close ties to those involved and promoted the asset only as a private individual, indicating direct negotiation with his circle, including a payment to his sister, Karina Milei. The Argentine Presidency reiterated it would not engage with journalistic versions, stating Milei had already explained himself publicly.

item.title: Papo Antagonista – Daniel Vorcaro surta e fará delação premiada
item.description: On Papo Antagonista, Daniel Vorcaro erupted and confirmed he signed a plea bargain deal, sparking political repercussions. The show also covered Jair Bolsonaro’s health, the legal victory of student Isadora Borges over Erika Hilton, a live rebuttal of Guilherme Boulos, and Cuba’s demographic situation with the return of part of its population.

item.title: Número de Nikolas aparece entre os contatos de Vorcaro
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee found the phone numbers of federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) among the contacts of banker Daniel Vorcado. Nikolas stated he does not recall communicating with Vorcado via phone or messages, though he acknowledged such contact is 'possible'.

item.title: De olho em cenário pós-eleitoral em SP, PL ‘rouba’ mais um deputado estadual do PSD
item.description: The PL intensified its strategy to expand its bench in the São Paulo State Assembly (Alesp) by welcoming state deputy Oséias de Madureira (ex-PSD), federal deputy Cezinha de Madureira, pastors Rogério Martins and Elias Amaral, and state deputy Marcelo Aguiar (ex-Podemos). The move aims to consolidate the PL as the largest parliamentary bloc in Alesp by 2026 to strengthen its position in the race to succeed André do Prado as president of the legislature.

item.title: Presidente da Bolívia associa narcotráfico a “terrorismo”
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz linked narcotrafficking activities to terrorism in an interview following a meeting with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at Itamaraty. He emphasized the arrest of Uruguayan narcotrafficker Sebastián Marset, an ally of the PCC, as a key step in reducing violence and instability in Bolivia, particularly in Santa Cruz de la Sierra. Paz stated that Bolivia faces the export of violence from Brazil and announced a cooperation agreement to combat transnational organized crime, including drug trafficking, money laundering, and cybercrimes.
